The context mentions that the endodermis has cells impregnated with hydrophobic substances (Casparian strip) to restrict apoplastic flow of water. This implies that certain plant cell walls can restrict water entry, but the question is general: "do plant cell walls restrict the entry of water?"  
In general, plant cell walls are permeable to water, so the answer is no — they do not restrict water entry. The restriction comes from specialized structures like the Casparian strip.
